what_it_does: Records a product decision to defer in-app image and file attachment functionality for the P9 cycle, explaining that blob:// storage alone is insufficient for the full mobile attachment UX and offering lighter alternatives for UI feedback.
observations: Item is a block noticeboard entry with id item-2edbcf40 and source claude_code.
It links via part_of to board://item-f9f4a6a6.
Title uses P9 and a BD-H label with a ❌ status marker.
Notes that blob:// exists but mobile pickers, MIME handling, thumbnails, and draw-on-image are not free.
Identifies the one valuable attachment case as UI feedback screenshots.
Proposes screenshots can be supplied through the photo library into a text comment or described in text.
Voice-note attachment is mentioned as even further out and additive.
standouts: Explicitly rejects building an in-app attachment flow now despite blob:// being available.
Treats screenshot feedback as satisfiable outside the app via photo-library-to-comment.
Voice-note attachment is flagged as a future additive feature tied to a user preference.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, created/updated timestamps, and history.
Markdown body has a level-3 heading echoing the title followed by explanatory prose.
Uses emoji status markers and a P9 / BD-H label in the title.
Declares a part_of relationship to a parent board item.
concerns: The BD-H label and the term P9 are not defined within the file.
'Loop is humming' and 'real need surfaces' are vague deferral criteria.
The identity/role of Tim is unexplained.
notes: A noticeboard item deferring in-app image and file attachments for P9, with a workaround of screenshots pasted into text comments.
questions: What does the BD-H label signify?
What is the scope and timeline of the P9 cycle?
Who is Tim and what is his role in the workflow?
What is the parent item-f9f4a6a6 and how does this item relate to its children?
What concrete conditions determine when the attachment flow should be reconsidered?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose—documenting the deferral of image and file attachment for P9—matches the content.
apparent_themes: Pragmatic user workarounds over built-in functionality
Awareness of hidden mobile UX costs
intention_signals: To keep the P9 cycle focused by avoiding a full attachment pipeline, while capturing a lightweight, acceptable path for visual UI feedback.
novelty: It explicitly counters the assumption that blob:// makes attachments free by enumerating the mobile picker, MIME, thumbnail, and annotation costs.
contribution: Preserves the decision context, rationale, and a temporary workaround for image/file feedback so the team does not re-litigate the deferral prematurely.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard block item documenting the P9 decision to defer in-app image and file attachment features, noting that blob:// storage does not cover mobile picker, MIME, thumbnail, and draw-on-image costs, and suggesting screenshot-via-photo-library or text description as a short-term UI feedback path.
intention_for_embedding: To record and communicate the rationale for deferring attachment flows in favor of a leaner P9 build.
